published_prior_art.cite: CinelliHazlett2020 + ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019 + AdjahoChristensen2025 supply component sensitivity languages, not this unified policy-score display

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1: sharp joint-misspecification interval and non-rectangularity witness, not a CH/MSM/covariate-shift calibration frontier

published_prior_art.cite: JinXuZhengZhou2025 and AdjahoChristensen2025 give named robust-policy criteria, but not the all-pairs common-scale diagnostic
HuangPimentel2023 and SorianoBenMichaelBickelFellerPimentel2023 discuss weighting sensitivity, not an open-set two-policy penalty-separation witness

strengthening_paths.suggestion: State explicitly in Conjecture 1 that the named frontier is between the Jin-Xu-Zheng-Zhou MSM robust-welfare criterion and the Adjaho-Christensen external-validity policy-choice criterion, with CH or variance-based weighting as the scalar reporting language.
Add a minimal two-policy finite-support example in Section 9 or Section 10 showing equal L2 penalties but unequal D1 or covariate-projection penalties, even if the full open-set proof is deferred.
Sharpen the observable-status claim for W_pi by adding the exact local normalization under which the Gateaux score is recoverable from P and nominal nuisance inputs.

recommended_next_step: Proceed to Stage 0 with Conjecture 1 as the flagship and keep Theorem 1 as a routine support-function lemma.
